purpose: Reconcile marketed-product, pipeline and acquisition claims while keeping unsupported probabilities explicit.

primary_sources.title: Biogen Schedule TO / Apellis offer and CVR terms
Biogen Form 8-K (Apellis closing)
primary_sources.note: Acquisition cost approximately $5.3bn excluding fees/CVRs; $2bn term facilities fully drawn; max CVR payments estimated ~$582m per local 8-K extraction.

resolved_facts: 2025 total revenue was $9.891 billion: $7.119 billion product revenue, $1.861 billion anti-CD20 revenue, $177.7 million Alzheimer's collaboration revenue and $732.9 million contract manufacturing, royalty and other revenue.
2025 MS product revenue was $4.039 billion, down 7.1%; rare-disease product revenue was $2.154 billion, up 8.4%; biosimilars were $729.1 million, down 8.1%.

Growth products increased 19% in 2025. VUMERITY revenue was $746.8 million; QALSODY was $86.9 million; SPINRAZA U.S. revenue was $625.5 million and management expected global SPINRAZA revenue to be roughly flat in 2026.

TECFIDERA faces deeply discounted generics and its relevant European patent was revoked in November 2025; the annual report states revenue is expected to continue declining. Listed U.S. TYSABRI method patents include 2026 and 2027 expirations.

2025 operating cash flow was approximately $2.2 billion, capex $154 million and free cash flow approximately $2.1 billion. Year-end cash was about $4.2 billion and debt about $6.3 billion before the Apellis closing.

Litifilimab's two Phase 3 SLE studies were expected to read out in second-half 2026; CLE Phase 3 was expected in 2027. Approval can trigger up to $250 million of fixed milestones to Royalty Pharma plus a mid-single-digit royalty.
Biogen completed the Apellis acquisition on May 14, 2026. Upfront consideration was $41 per Apellis share, approximately $5.6 billion in aggregate, financed with cash and borrowings.

Each Apellis CVR can pay $2 if annual SYFOVRE-related sales reach $1.5 billion in 2027-2030 and another $2 at $2.0 billion in 2027-2030; a special $4 payment applies if neither earlier payment occurs and $2.0 billion is reached in 2031. The acquired products generated $689 million in 2025 revenue.

gap_assessments.reconciliation: Revenue by portfolio and selected products, collaboration economics, patent dates, debt, cash flow and aggregate expenses are available. Product-level rebates, contribution margins, sustaining commercial investment and post-exclusivity tails are not fully disclosed. The $35/$55/$75 legacy and $25/$55/$95 growth-product per-share ranges are not reproducible from the packet.
gap_assessments.overlap_control: Collaboration shares, royalties and commercial/R&D costs must be deducted at the product level; Apellis products are excluded from Biogen's pre-close growth-products component.
gap_assessments.falsifier: Legacy erosion accelerates beyond the modeled tail or growth products fail to offset it after net collaboration and commercial costs.
gap_assessments.monitoring_source: Quarterly product revenue, collaboration profit sharing, cost of sales, SG&A, patent litigation and launch metrics.
gap_assessments.unresolved: Product-level net cash-flow schedules and scenario-specific exclusivity dates remain analyst work, not primary facts.

gap_assessments.reconciliation: Programs, phase, several readout dates and selected contingent obligations are disclosed. Independent indication-level clinical base rates, probability dependencies, remaining R&D, launch capital and failure values are not reconciled. The $20/$60/$130 pipeline and $20/$45/$75 Apellis per-share ranges remain judgmental.
gap_assessments.overlap_control: Approved Apellis products belong only in the Apellis asset component; new indications are separate event branches. A shared molecule's probabilities and costs must not be duplicated across indications.
gap_assessments.falsifier: Phase 3 efficacy/safety misses, delayed filings, adverse label/reimbursement, or commercial uptake below the event-tree threshold reduce the relevant branch to failure or delay value.
gap_assessments.monitoring_source: ClinicalTrials.gov, FDA action dates, company trial releases, SEC milestone/royalty notes and quarterly R&D disclosures.
gap_assessments.unresolved: The packet lacks independently sourced base rates and complete remaining-cost schedules for every material indication.

gap_assessments.reconciliation: May 14 2026 closing 8-K adds: acquisition cost approximately $5.3bn excluding fees/CVRs, $2bn term facilities fully drawn, maximum CVR payments estimated at about $582m. Upfront/CVR contract terms were already known. Post-close purchase-price allocation, assumed liabilities, integration costs, and day-one cash still require the first post-close 10-Q. The -$70/-$55/-$40 per-share senior-claims range cannot yet be independently reproduced.
gap_assessments.overlap_control: Existing net debt, acquisition borrowings, CVR expected value, integration cost and required liquidity are deducted once; CVR payments cannot also reduce the Apellis product value if already modeled there.
gap_assessments.falsifier: Post-close debt or integration cash costs exceed the modeled claims, deleveraging misses the stated trajectory, or CVR liability is understated relative to product assumptions.
gap_assessments.monitoring_source: Second-quarter 2026 Form 10-Q, acquisition footnote, debt footnote, purchase-price allocation, integration disclosures and CVR liability remeasurement.
gap_assessments.unresolved: The first post-close 10-Q had not been filed as of this evidence date; the closing balance sheet therefore remains a decision-critical blocker.

committee_conclusion: The acquisition contract and major reported anchors are reconciled, but product cash flows, clinical probabilities and the post-close balance sheet are not. Approval is not supportable; committee work should terminate as evidence-blocked pending the Q2 filing and explicit event trees.
